subject: Chemistry
title: Flame Tests: Colors from Metal Ions
description: One-minute skill: Flame Tests: Colors from Metal Ions.

hook.text: Hold different metal salts over a flame: sodium burns orange, copper burns green, lithium burns red.

buildup.text: Heat excites electrons in metal atoms, bumping them to higher energy levels.

discovery.text: When electrons fall back down, they emit photons of specific wavelengths—visible as distinct colors.

twist.text: Every element has a unique spectrum. That's how astronomers identify what distant stars are made of.

climax.text: Fireworks use this same principle. Strontium for red, barium for green, copper for blue.

punchline.text: Heat metal, electrons jump, light appears. Each element has its own color.

quiz.question: Why do different metals produce different flame colors?
options: Each element's electrons emit photons at unique wavelengths
Different metals burn at different temperatures
The flames reflect the metal's natural color
